message:
test: pin sys/epoll.h conformance via differential harness

Adds tests/conformance_diff_epoll.rs covering 4 functions:
- epoll_create / epoll_create1: creation, EPOLL_CLOEXEC, invalid flags
- epoll_ctl ADD: register fd for EPOLLIN, then verify wait returns it
- epoll_ctl DEL: unregister, then verify wait sees no events
- epoll_wait: readable detection on socketpair, timeout-no-events

Each test uses isolated fl-only / lc-only epoll instances so
registration state doesn't bleed across impls.

6 tests pass, 0 divergences against glibc.

Bead: bd-epol1

 {"id":"bd-epeg","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_kernel crate integration (Cx, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId)","description":"Integrate FrankenLibC with franken_kernel suite substrate: adopt canonical Cx capability context, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion types. All TSM decisions must flow through Cx. All evidence records must use canonical IDs. Must not fork franken_kernel schema. ADOPTION WEDGE: FrankenLibC must function standalone without franken_kernel (fallback to local types).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:16.769752763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.645348954Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.645135059Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption-wedge","cross-crate","franken-kernel","frankenlibc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1293,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Started a bounded `bd-epeg` slice by extending the local fallback ID/schema contract into `alien_cs_metrics`.

Changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/alien_cs_metrics.rs` to import the local fallback `DecisionId` and `MEMBRANE_SCHEMA_VERSION`.
- Metric event JSONL rows now emit `decision_id` and `schema_version`.
- Aggregate snapshot JSONL rows now emit `decision_id: 0` and `schema_version`.
- Tightened the Alien CS JSONL tests to require those fields and to assert deterministic values.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`
  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane alien_cs_metrics -- --nocapture`
  Expected: alien_cs_metrics targeted tests pass with the updated JSONL contract.
  Result: passed, 14 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- `bv --robot-triage/--robot-next/--robot-insights` still ranks the TSM epic as the strongest actionable lane. This bead was the next concrete, useful step because it extends canonical fallback IDs into another structured evidence exporter without waiting for full `franken_kernel` integration.
- Agent Mail inbox remained empty and the project currently shows only `MistyGrove` registered, so there were no peer agents to notify directly.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:42:31Z"},{"id":1294,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added a second `bd-epeg` slice by aligning the HJI proof artifact schema tag with the shared fallback membrane schema.

Initial test expectations surfaced a real exporter-contract mismatch (pressure rows use symbol runtime_math::pressure_sensor and path pressure_sensor::observe; overload rows use symbol runtime_math::degradation_policy and path pressure_sensor::degradation_policy), so I corrected the regression to the actual emitted contract and reran. Starting the next bounded slice now by tightening another weak exporter regression in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-16T13:27:16Z"},{"id":1316,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Tightened `runtime_kernel_framework_exports_runtime_math_jsonl_logs` in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs. Verified with rch: env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_exports_snapshot_regret_and_drift_alerts -- --nocapture; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields -- --nocapture. cargo check succeeded with the usual non-fatal rsync code-24 artifact retrieval warning after exit=0 on the remote worker.","created_at":"2026-03-17T00:35:19Z"},{"id":1319,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Re-ran bv --format json --robot-triage / --robot-next / --robot-insights; top graph still points to bd-2vv and bd-32e, so I am continuing the actionable bd-epeg membrane evidence lane. Starting the next bounded slice in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs: add a deterministic regression for runtime_snapshot_field_out_of_range rows so invalid cached snapshot values must emit canonical JSONL warning rows with the shared schema/trace envelope.","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:43:39Z"},{"id":1320,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added another deterministic bd-epeg runtime-math exporter regression in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs for runtime_snapshot_field_out_of_range rows. The new test forces invalid cached pressure/policy snapshot values, exports JSONL, and requires one canonical warning row per invalid field with schema_version=1.0, runtime_math::snapshot::range trace scope, bead/scenario IDs, decision_path=snapshot::validate_fields, artifact_refs, and exact observed/min/max values for pressure_regime_code, pressure_score_milli, pressure_raw_score_milli, and overload_policy_tag. Verified via rch: env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_exports_snapshot_field_range_violations -- --nocapture; env TMPDIR=/data/tmp cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_exports_snapshot_regret_and_drift_alerts -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-17T01:46:51Z"},{"id":1321,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Re-ran bv --format json --robot-triage / --robot-next / --robot-insights; top graph still points to bd-2vv and bd-32e, so I am continuing the actionable bd-epeg runtime-math evidence lane.  {"id":"bd-g2bd5","title":"[CRITICAL] glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption under default parallelism (free(): invalid size)","description":"## Symptom

`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` aborts with:

```
free(): invalid size
error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`
...
process didn't exit successfully: ...glibc_internal_abi_test-... (signal: 6, SIGABRT)
```

Reproduced on two independent workers (vmi1149989, vmi1156319) with current main tip 7c9b3127 on 2026-04-22.

## Key diagnostic

The same binary with `--test-threads=1` **passes all 172 tests cleanly** (finishes in 0.04s). Corruption only manifests when the default rayon/cargo test parallelism is enabled. That narrows the bug to:

- a data race on a heap-allocated global / thread-local
- two tests freeing the same allocation through different paths
- or a reallocation that trusts a size field modified concurrently

## Location hint

Failure in the two runs landed near these test names (the test that actually freed the corrupt pointer is not necessarily the one that emitted the abort — whichever thread allocated last is what glibc trips on):

run 1 (vmi1149989): last 'ok' line was `resolv_context_native_shim_reuses_tls_context_and_preserves_errno`, then `free(): invalid size`.

run 2 (vmi1156319): last 'ok' line was `test_idna_from_dns_null_input`, then `free(): invalid size`.

Cluster of tests running around the failure: TLS dtors (`test_call_tls_dtors_*`), IDNA (`test_idna_*`), resolv context shims (`resolv_context_native_shim_*`, `resolv_context_override_*`). These all touch thread-local state / TLS dtor lists and share a registry.

## Fix direction

1. Rerun with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 MALLOC_CHECK_=2` to get a glibc backtrace pointing at the freeing site.
2. Audit the TLS-dtor list, resolv-context override pool, and IDNA allocation paths for any `Box::from_raw` / `free` on a pointer whose size metadata is modified elsewhere without a lock.
3. Add `#[ignore]` or `serial_test::serial` gate on the implicated tests only after the root cause is understood (applying `serial` prematurely masks the bug instead of fixing it).

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace` from completing cleanly — the very first workspace-wide test abort halts CI before later crates (frankenlibc-core, frankenlibc-membrane, frankenlibc-harness, frankenlibc-bench) ever run. Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE pass.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` reproduces reliably on two distinct workers.
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test -- --test-threads=1` passes (172/172, 0.04s).
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.

 {"id":"bd-gcy","title":"bd-c1x subtask: Futex condvar core implementation (wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait)","description":"Background:
- Condvar implementation must coordinate with mutex ownership and futex wake ordering safely.

Goal:
- Implement futex-based condvar core with deterministic sequence and wake semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Internal condvar sequence/counter design.
2) wait path with unlock/relock choreography around futex_wait.
3) signal/broadcast wake policy.
4) Timed wait with absolute deadline handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Core condvar operations satisfy spec for success/error/timeout cases.
- No missed wakeups in deterministic stress tests.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests: signal-before-wait, wait-before-signal, broadcast-many, timeout races.
- Structured logs: trace_id, mode, condvar_state, mutex_state, wake_count, timeout_state, errno.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHawk","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.531693883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:32.207008001Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:32.206927771Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","implementation","pthread","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1088,"issue_id":"bd-gcy","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Completed futex condvar core implementation (bd-gcy). Deliverables:

1) CondvarData repr(C) struct with seq counter, waiter count, associated mutex, and clock_id — overlays onto pthread_cond_t memory (20 bytes, fits in 48-byte struct).

2) condvar_wait(): atomic unlock-wait-relock choreography using sys_futex. Captures seq before unlock, parks with FUTEX_WAIT on seq word, relocks via CAS+futex loop on return. Validates mutex association invariant (EINVAL on mismatch).

3) condvar_timedwait(): uses FUTEX_WAIT_BITSET with absolute timeout. FUTEX_CLOCK_REALTIME flag selects clock domain matching condvar's configured clock. Returns ETIMEDOUT on deadline expiry after relocking mutex (per POSIX).

4) condvar_signal(): increments seq + futex_wake(1). condvar_broadcast(): increments seq + futex_wake(INT_MAX). Both are no-ops when no waiters (seq still incremented for consistency).

5) relock_mutex() helper: simple CAS loop with futex parking for relocking after condvar wait.

6) 64 total tests pass: 41 contract tests (bd-blg) + 23 core implementation tests including 4 threaded integration tests:
   - core_condvar_wait_signal_roundtrip (single waiter + signal)
   - core_condvar_broadcast_wakes_all (4 waiters + broadcast)
   - core_condvar_timedwait_expires (past deadline -> ETIMEDOUT)
   - core_condvar_mutex_association_mismatch (EINVAL on different mutex)

7) Exports from pthread module: CondvarData, condvar_init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast.

Verification: cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::cond (64 passed, 0 failed). cargo check --all-targets clean.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-sq7ae","title":"atfork runners hold Mutex across user callbacks — self-deadlock on re-entrant pthread_atfork","description":"run_atfork_prepare/parent/child in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:4350-4385 hold ATFORK_HANDLERS.lock() across arbitrary user-callback invocation. A prepare/parent/child handler that transitively calls pthread_atfork (common in lazy-init library paths) re-locks a non-recursive std::sync::Mutex on the same thread -> deadlock. Concrete interleaving: T1 fork() -> run_atfork_prepare() holds lock -> calls H1 -> H1 init'd some library L2 that registers its own handler via pthread_atfork -> pthread_atfork tries ATFORK_HANDLERS.lock() -> self-deadlock. Class 1 (classic mutex deadlock). Fix: snapshot handlers (Vec<AtforkHandlers> with derive(Clone, Copy)), drop guard, iterate snapshot. Affects: unistd_abi.rs:5415/5432/5434 fork interposer and process_abi.rs:139/156/159 fork interposer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:26:44.711167663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:40:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: snapshot ATFORK_HANDLERS under lock, iterate snapshot unlocked. Regression test added in tests/pthread_abi_test.rs. Landed in 437e3d0d.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-stlnv","title":"FUZZ: ioctl command-code structure-aware harness","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_ioctl.rs. ioctl has ~hundreds of command codes, each with different third-arg semantics (int, pointer-to-struct, etc.) — a classic confused-deputy surface. Asserts no panic regardless of (fd, cmd, arg).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:17:20.475850155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T05:20:05.989296195Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T05:20:05.987414092Z","close_reason":"Added structure-aware ioctl fuzz harness and registered fuzz_ioctl; verified with rch cargo test --manifest-path crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_ioctl -- -runs=1.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sttk9","title":"fuzz: read/write/pread/pwrite buffer + count validation","description":"## Scope
Add crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_read_write.rs targeting frankenlibc_abi::unistd_abi::{read,write,pread,pwrite,readv,writev}. Focus on buffer-bound validation + short-read/short-write semantics + errno correctness for invalid fd/len/buf.

## Archetype
1 + 3: Crash-detector + **differential** against libc for matched calls, using a seeded pipe pair and /dev/zero / /dev/null fds as deterministic backends.

## Inputs
struct ReadWriteFuzzInput {
  op: u8,                 // 0=read, 1=write, 2=pread, 3=pwrite, 4=readv, 5=writev
  fd_selector: u8,        // 0=/dev/zero, 1=/dev/null, 2=pipe-read-end, 3=pipe-write-end, 4=closed-fd, 5=-1
  buf_len: u16,           // 0..=32768
  buf_offset: u8,         // shift within a larger allocation (exercises decide's address checks)
  poison_ptr: bool,       // NULL or address we can catch
  offset: i64,            // for pread/pwrite
  iovec_count: u8,        // 0..=8 for readv/writev
}

## Invariants
- No panic on any input
- rc is a non-negative byte count OR -1 with errno in {EBADF, EFAULT, EINVAL, EIO, EAGAIN, EINTR, EPIPE, ENOSPC, ESPIPE, EOVERFLOW}
- Host parity for the /dev/zero, /dev/null, closed-fd cases: ours matches libc::read/write bit-for-bit
- readv/writev total byte count equals the sum of per-iovec byte counts
- Buffer is not read/written past requested len (ASAN + explicit sentinel check before and after)

## Expected bug classes
- Off-by-one in iovec loop, iov_len overflow, fd validation bypass, membrane decision mismatches for cross-page buffers, missing EINTR retry, sign handling for large counts (SSIZE_MAX), ESPIPE not mapped for pread/pwrite on pipes

## Sentinel pattern
Allocate 2*buf_len + 2 pages, place guard bytes (0xAA) before and after the requested window; after the call assert guard bytes unchanged.

## Score
5 × 5 × /2 ≈ 12.5 — tied with bd-o4yda for priority.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:39:39.154205881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:22.578845312Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:21:22.576538966Z","close_reason":"fuzz_read_write harness committed and green: 16.3M execs / 108K exec/s in 151s. Covers read, write, pread, pwrite, readv, writev against 5 deterministic fd backends (/dev/zero, /dev/null, pipe read, pipe write, closed fd, wild fd). Sentinel-byte guards before/after every buffer window catch any over-count write. Differential parity against libc for non-pipe backends. Two harness-side bugs fixed during bring-up (count > buf_size overrun, buf_offset into post-pad). Zero real impl bugs under the invariant set — the read/write wrapper is solid. Committed 4e263808.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sttk9","depends_on_id":"bd-jy4qu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:01:44.052743346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
